option.hypothesis: Vectorizing the inner loop of `build_kernel` to process VLEN=8 elements per iteration, while reusing constant registers for loop indices and eliminating per-element scratch allocation, will reduce instruction count and pipeline stalls.

change_boundary.minimality_claim: Modifies only the inner loop generation logic and constant handling within build_kernel, preserving outer loop structure, hash computation, and memory access patterns.

causal_evidence.claim: Baseline metric shows 147734 cycles; scratch_const is invoked per element causing redundant load instructions; scalar loop overhead dominates execution time.

invariants: preserved memory access patterns for forest_values
preserved scratch_const behavior for non-loop constants.

fallback.on_failure: Revert to scalar loop but retain constant register reuse and scratch allocation optimization.

option.why_not_smaller: The optimization spans the entire inner loop body and constant management; splitting would eliminate the batching and register reuse benefits that drive the performance gain.
